page: Google Cloud Platform g4-standard-24 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 90.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 3.75 GB per vCPU. It includes a dedicated GPU allocation consisting of one NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 graphics processor to accelerate parallel computing tasks.

This server configuration does not include local NVMe SSD storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage options. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g4-standard-24 provides a balanced allocation of dedicated CPU and memory resources alongside professional-grade GPU acceleration, making it a structured option for workloads requiring hardware acceleration without the overhead of massive memory footprints.

Appropriate workloads for the g4-standard-24 include hardware-accelerated graphics rendering, 3D modeling, machine learning inference, and general-purpose GPU-accelerated compute tasks that fit within the 90.0 GB system memory boundary.
description: Google Cloud Platform g4-standard-24 is a graphics-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 90.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core. The hardware profile is equipped with one NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 GPU to support accelerated computing workloads. This instance does not include local storage, requiring external block storage solutions, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The resource distribution offers a balanced ratio of compute and memory alongside dedicated GPU resources, presenting a qualitative balance for hardware-accelerated tasks. It is designed for workloads such as graphics rendering, 3D modeling, and GPU-accelerated data processing.
